headlines.text: Trump departs Beijing after summit with Xi; touts 'fantastic' trade deals with no major breakthroughs announced
Iran FM doubts US 'seriousness' in nuke talks; ceasefire hanging by a thread on Day 77
Drake drops THREE albums at midnight: Iceman, Habibti, and Maid of Honour flood streaming platforms
S&P 500 and Nasdaq slide Friday as tech stocks give back gains; Dow retreats from 50,000
Gas prices hit $4.53 national average — highest level since 2022 — as Iran war constricts global oil supply
Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next Fed Chair as Jerome Powell's term ends today
House Iran war powers resolution fails 212-212 in dramatic tied vote
Ship seized near Strait of Hormuz as tensions flare; another vessel sunk in the region
South Carolina governor calls special session to eliminate sole Democratic House seat in redistricting push
Starbucks lays off 300 more corporate workers in third round of cuts, warns of $400M restructuring charges

breaking.text: BREAKING: Ship seized near Strait of Hormuz as Iran tensions spike Friday morning

stories.hed: Iran FM Says US Isn't Serious; Ceasefire Creaks; Ships Get Grabbed Near Hormuz
stories.dek: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced he doubts America's 'seriousness' in negotiations and confirmed talks with Russia about moving enriched uranium — which is either diplomacy or a very alarming game of hot potato. Meanwhile a ship was seized near the Strait of Hormuz on Friday morning, because apparently the waterway is now a lawless parking lot.

stories.hed: Trump Leaves Beijing Claiming Wins; Xi Made Clear Taiwan Is His Top Priority
stories.dek: President Trump departed China after two days of talks at the Great Hall of the People, calling results 'fantastic' while analysts noted no major binding agreements were signed. Xi reportedly warned that mishandling Taiwan could cause 'clashes and even conflicts,' which is diplomatic code for 'do not touch.'

stories.hed: Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair as Jerome Powell Exits Stage Left
stories.dek: The Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve just as markets were tanking on Friday, which is the kind of timing that builds character. Treasury yields hit near one-year highs at 4.55% as markets priced in a possible rate hike — odds that went from 1% to 45% in a single month of war-driven inflation.

stories.hed: House Iran War Powers Vote Ties 212-212, Fails — A Literal Coin Flip on War Authority
stories.dek: In a rare tied vote, the House failed to pass a resolution requiring congressional approval for further military action against Iran, with three Republicans breaking ranks to join nearly all Democrats. A tied vote counts as a loss, meaning Trump's war powers remain unchecked, and Rep. Jared Golden of Maine became the only Democrat who apparently had other plans.

stories.hed: Republicans Are Gerrymandering So Hard the Supreme Court Had to Help — Voters Are the Collateral Damage
stories.dek: With midterms approaching and the Supreme Court having weakened the Voting Rights Act, GOP-led states are sprinting to redraw maps in a redistricting blitz that voting experts say will cost voters dearly regardless of which party wins. South Carolina is eliminating its only Democratic House seat, and that's just a Friday in 2026.

markets.context: Markets sold off Friday as Trump's China summit ended with no Iran breakthrough, spiking oil prices and Treasury yields while tech stocks gave back recent record-high gains.

gas.note: Filling up your tank now costs roughly what a fancy brunch did last year — except this hurts more and no one brings you mimosas.

popCulture.hed: Drake Drops Three Albums Simultaneously at Midnight: Iceman, Habibti, and Maid of Honour
popCulture.dek: In an unprecedented music industry move, Drake released 43 songs across three full albums at midnight on May 15, after months of viral ice-sculpture stunts and livestream episodes in Toronto teasing just one. Whether this is a masterclass in saturation marketing or a cry for help is a debate currently raging on every corner of the internet.

genZ.definition: Drake releasing three albums simultaneously at midnight on May 15 — Iceman, Habibti, and Maid of Honour — after a months-long campaign involving a 25-foot ice sculpture in Toronto that fans literally attacked with pickaxes to find the release date. The internet is now debating whether dropping 43 songs at once is genius or just Drake flooding the zone so at least something has to hit.
genZ.usage: Iran ceasefire negotiations rn are giving major Drake Triple Drop energy — overwhelming volume of content, nothing quite landing, and everyone's exhausted but still hitting play.

tldr: Trump left Beijing empty-handed on Iran while the ceasefire frayed further on Day 77, ships got seized near Hormuz, and stocks dropped as oil spiked and investors wondered if the war is about to resume. On the bright side, Drake dropped three albums at midnight, which is either the most or least important thing that happened today depending entirely on your priorities.
